paper_title: Turtle Fossils and Trace Fossils Analysis

methods.description: This method involves the careful analysis of turtle fossils to identify evidence of parasite, scavenger, or predator behavior. The analysis is conducted on fossils from the Division of Fossil Primates at the Duke Lemur Center and the Natural History Museum of London.

steps.description: All available turtle fossils in the collections of the Division of Fossil Primates at the Duke Lemur Center and in the Natural History Museum of London were carefully analyzed.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch and analyze turtle fossils from specified collections
inputs: turtle fossils from Duke Lemur Center
turtle fossils from Natural History Museum of London

steps.description: Analyze fossils for evidence of parasite, scavenger, or predator behavior.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and document evidence of biological interactions on fossils

methods.description: This method involves analyzing modified osteological remains from an ichnological perspective to determine if markings, pits, grooves, and lesions were emplaced by external agents or organisms.

steps.description: Analyze the material from an ichnological perspective to determine if markings, pits, grooves, lesions, etc., were emplaced by external agents/organisms.
steps.searchable_summary: determine origin of markings on fossils from external agents
outputs: classification of markings as trace fossils
